title: Fixing the LinkedList Add at Head

statement.markdown: Given the head of a singly linked list, implement a function that adds a new node at the beginning of the list.
statement.inputFormat: Head of a singly linked list
statement.notes: Time complexity: O(1), Space complexity: O(1)

examples.explanation: Add 1 at the beginning of the list 2 3

hints.text: Remember to update the next pointers of the nodes

editorial.title: Fixing the LinkedList Add at Head
editorial.intuition: Create a new node with the given data and update the next pointer of the head node to point to the new node.
editorial.bruteForce: Iterate through the list to find the correct position to insert the new node.
editorial.optimalSolution: Update the next pointer of the head node directly.
commonMistakes: Incorrectly updating the next pointers of the nodes

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: ListNode* addAtHead(ListNode* head, int val); };
starterCode.java: class Solution { public ListNode addAtHead(ListNode head, int val); };
starterCode.python: class Solution: def addAtHead(self, head: ListNode, val: int) -> ListNode:

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: ListNode* addAtHead(ListNode* head, int val) { if (head == nullptr) { return new ListNode(val); } head->val = val; return head->next; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: class Solution { public ListNode addAtHead(ListNode head, int val) { if (head == null) { return new ListNode(val); } head.val = val; return head.next; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def addAtHead(self, head: ListNode, val: int) -> ListNode: if head is None: return ListNode(val) head.val = val return head.next
